design_philosophy.core_loop: Master weapon loadouts and boss attack patterns through repeated attempts; cycle between short, easy stages and intensely challenging boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Memorization and execution of boss-specific strategies versus resource management (ammo, health, positioning); timing of defensive counter moves against incoming fire
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Epsilon-Eagle, a genetically engineered super-soldier with multiple weapons and special abilities, fighting through a terrorist organization's hierarchy in a sci-fi revenge narrative
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly designed for 'hardcore' Mega Drive players; prioritizes boss fights over traditional level design; intentionally short stages serve as downtime between 26 boss encounters; player must pre-select weapon loadouts from 6 options, forcing meaningful strategic choices before each run; eschews passwords and unlimited continues on hard difficulty to enforce commitment

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system; lost upon death
difficulty.continues_system: Unlimited continues available on 'Supereasy' difficulty; no continues on 'Superhard' difficulty, making it a single continuous attempt
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints within levels; password system only available on easier difficulty
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Two explicit difficulty modes ('Supereasy' and 'Superhard'); player can pre-select which 4 weapons to equip from 6 total options, creating loadout-based difficulty variance; choice between fixed-fire aiming (immobile, precise) versus free-moving style (mobile, less accurate) affects difficulty perception

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon selection system: 6 distinct weapons (Buster, Ranger, Flame, Homing, Sword, Lancer) with different damage profiles, ranges, and ammo costs; player equips 4 before run starts. Ammo replenishment between encounters. Special moves unlock through gameplay mastery: Counter Force (converts enemy fire to health), Phoenix Force (full-health dash attack with invulnerability and slow-motion damage), Zero Force (fallback weak attack when ammo depleted)
progression.unlock_mechanism: No traditional unlocks; all weapons available for pre-game loadout customization from the start
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon combinations create different run experiences
Two distinct difficulty modes with different rule sets
Boss patterns reward memorization across multiple attempts
26 different boss encounters with unique vulnerability patterns
Speedrun optimization potential through advanced techniques (lancer switch trick, counter force abuse, phoenix edge-screen abuse)

world_building_techniques: Long text scroll opening exposition explaining the A-Humans, Scarlet organization, and Epsilon-Eagle's assassination attempt and resurrection
Story explicitly dropped after opening; not referenced during gameplay
Boss encounters serve as implicit narrative progression (fighting through Scarlet's hierarchy to reach Xi-Tiger)
Character archetype naming (Xi-Tiger, Epsilon-Eagle) suggests anime-inspired sci-fi world

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters are the primary design focus; each boss presents a distinct damage vulnerability profile requiring players to identify optimal weapon choices and positioning strategies. Bosses serve as skill-gating mechanisms testing mastery of movement (hovering, double-jumping, counter-force air control), weapon selection, and pattern recognition. Short stages between bosses exist solely as 'downtime' to replenish ammo and practice movement
notable_bosses: Bosses feature multiple vulnerable parts with different damage multipliers per weapon
Some bosses have organic components (weak to Flame) and mechanical components (Flame ineffective)
Lancer weapon noted as highest single-shot damage (4160 RAM), suggesting certain bosses designed as Lancer checks

faction_indicators: Scarlet organization: terrorist group seeking planetary domination
Xi-Tiger faction: splinter group that became 'too brutal even for Scarlet'
Epsilon-Eagle's dual personality: good vs. evil split after resurrection, pursuing Xi across stages
danger_signals: Kidnapping and experimentation on children with special abilities
Hostage-taking and murder to force confrontation
Space-time breach caused by combat, suggesting technology/power scale concerns
environmental_storytelling: A-Human research laboratory location used as confrontation site
Stages imply Scarlet territorial control (must traverse their domain to reach leadership)
Boss progression implicitly charts player's path through organization hierarchy

sources_used: wikipedia (Alien Soldier article - gameplay mechanics, plot, development context, difficulty modes, weapon system)
tasvideos (weapon damage tables, advanced techniques, boss vulnerability patterns, movement mechanics)
